meta.description: Windows Calculator (Modern UWP). Single-session app — one instance at a time. Use RESET to clear state before reuse instead of closing and relaunching. Standard mode buttons shown here; other modes (Scientific, Programmer) add extra controls.

Single-session UWP calculator. One process, one window. Numeric buttons use AutomationId numXButton (X = 0–9). Operator buttons: plusButton, minusButton, multiplyButton, divideButton, equalButton. Clear buttons: clearButton (AC — clears all), clearEntryButton (CE — clears entry), backSpaceButton (backspace last digit). Result display: CalculatorResults (click or READ to get result string like "Zobrazuje se 32"). Expression display: CalculatorExpression (shows full expression like "4 × 8 ="). TextContainer inside CalculatorResults shows just the number: "32". RESET: clicks clearButton (or clearEntryButton); falls back to Ctrl+Z×20. Always call LISTWINDOWS first — reuse existing calculator window; never KILL.

binary.note: Hash intentionally blank — update after capture

Group.label: Display
Group.note: Read-only controls that show calculator state. Use READ or inspect Name attribute.

Control.label: Result / answer display. Shows the current expression result.

Read this to get the result after pressing equalButton. Value format localised, e.g. "Zobrazuje se 32" (Czech) or "Display is 32" (English). Parse the trailing number. Alternatively read TextContainer child for just the number.
Raw numeric display pane inside CalculatorResults.
Child of CalculatorResults. Name property is the pure numeric result ("32"). Query via QUERYTREE and read Name attribute — no action needed.
Expression display — shows the current in-progress expression, e.g. "4 × 8 =".
Shows the full expression being built. Use READ on the window to read both expression and result together. Generally not needed for read-back — use CalculatorResults or TextContainer for the answer.

Group.label: Clear / Correction
Group.note: Three levels of erasure: AC (all), CE (entry only), Backspace (one digit). All CLICKID.

Control.label: Clear / AC — clears both current entry and the expression.
Click to fully reset the calculator state. Equivalent to pressing C or AC. Preferred target for RESET fallback. After clicking, CalculatorResults shows 0 and CalculatorExpression is blank.
Clear Entry / CE — clears only the most-recently entered value.
Use to erase a mistyped digit/number without losing the pending operator. Difference from clearButton: clearEntryButton leaves the operator and first operand in place; clearButton resets everything.
Backspace — removes the last entered digit.
Removes one digit at a time from the current entry. Equivalent to {BACK} key.

Group.label: Digit buttons (0–9, decimal, negate)
Group.members: num0Button num1Button num2Button num3Button num4Button num5Button num6Button num7Button num8Button num9Button decimalSeparatorButton negateButton
Group.note: AutomationId pattern: numXButton. Also decimalSeparatorButton, negateButton (±).

Control.label: Digit 0
Type digit 0. CLICKID num0Button.
Digit 1
Type digit 1. CLICKID num1Button.
Digit 2
Type digit 2. CLICKID num2Button.
Digit 3
Type digit 3. CLICKID num3Button.
Digit 4
Type digit 4. CLICKID num4Button.
Digit 5
Type digit 5. CLICKID num5Button.
Digit 6
Type digit 6. CLICKID num6Button.
Digit 7
Type digit 7. CLICKID num7Button.
Digit 8
Type digit 8. CLICKID num8Button.
Digit 9
Type digit 9. CLICKID num9Button.
Decimal point / separator
Type decimal separator (locale-dependent: '.' or ','). CLICKID decimalSeparatorButton.
Negate — toggle positive/negative on current entry.
Applies ± to the current entry. CLICKID negateButton.

Group.label: Basic operators (+, −, ×, ÷, =)
Group.note: Binary operators expect a first operand, an operator click, a second operand, then equalButton.

Control.label: Addition (+)
Press after first operand to begin addition. CLICKID plusButton.
Subtraction (−)
Press after first operand to begin subtraction. CLICKID minusButton.
Multiplication (×)
Press after first operand to begin multiplication. CLICKID multiplyButton.
Division (÷)
Press after first operand to begin division. CLICKID divideButton.
Equals (=) — evaluates the expression.
Press after second operand to compute the result. After pressing, CalculatorResults shows the answer. Alternatively send {ENTER} via SENDKEYS. CLICKID equalButton.

Group.label: Unary / standard functions (%, 1/x, x², √x)
Group.note: Operate on the current entry immediately — no second operand needed.

Control.label: Percent (%)
Convert current entry to percent. CLICKID percentButton.
Reciprocal (1/x)
Compute 1 ÷ current entry. CLICKID invertButton.
Square (x²)
Compute x² on current entry. CLICKID xpower2Button.
Square root (√x)
Compute √x on current entry. CLICKID squareRootButton.

Group.label: Memory (MS / MR / M+ / M− / MC)
Group.note: Single memory slot. Store (MS), Recall (MR), Add (M+), Subtract (M−), Clear (MC).

Control.label: Memory Store (MS) — save current value into memory.
Stores current display value into memory slot. CLICKID memButton.
Memory Recall (MR) — paste stored value into display.
Retrieves stored memory value into current entry. CLICKID MemRecall.
Memory Add (M+) — add current value to stored memory.
Adds current display value to the memory slot. CLICKID MemPlus.
Memory Subtract (M−) — subtract current value from stored memory.
Subtracts current display value from the memory slot. CLICKID MemMinus.
Memory Clear (MC) — erase the memory slot.
Clears the memory slot entirely. CLICKID ClearMemoryButton.

Group.label: Navigation / mode switch
Group.note: TogglePaneButton switches between Standard, Scientific, Programmer modes. Avoid during arithmetic.

Control.label: Open navigation pane (hamburger menu).
Opens the left navigation panel to switch modes (Standard, Scientific, Programmer, etc.). Usually leave this alone during standard arithmetic sequences. CLICKID TogglePaneButton.
